premise: After my husband’s sudden death, his $250,000 life insurance payout was meant to fund the lifetime care of our developmentally disabled adult son, but my sister-in-law—who had volunteered as his aide—forged my husband’s signature on a beneficiary change form and deposited the full amount into her own account. When an insurance statement tipped me off, I sued for fraud, obtained a $275,000 judgment with interest, got her convicted of elder exploitation, and now control the funds in a court-supervised special-needs trust for my son while her home-health aide license was permanently revoked.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Sister-in-Law Stole My Son's $250K Care Fund. So I Stripped Her License.
